OSPS-AC-01.01_justification:
The single maintainer's GitHub account has 2FA enabled (TOTP + hardware key). GitHub also enforces 2FA for every account touching this repo.

Evidence: https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/securing-your-account-with-two-factor-authentication-2fa
OSPS-AC-02.01_justification: GitHub's default: new collaborators inherit no repo permissions unless explicitly granted. This repo has no external collaborators — only the single maintainer.
OSPS-AC-03.01_justification:
Repository ruleset 17924860 enforces no direct commits and no non-fast-forward on the default branch. See Rules → Ruleset settings.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/rules
OSPS-AC-03.02_justification:
Same ruleset also prevents deletion of the default branch.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/rules
OSPS-AC-04.01_justification:
Every workflow declares an explicit least-privilege permissions: block (e.g. release.yml grants only contents/id-token/attestations/packages: write; ci.yml grants contents: read).

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/tree/main/.github/workflows
OSPS-AC-04.02_justification:
Every actions/* invocation is pinned to a commit SHA with a trailing version comment; verified by OpenSSF Scorecard PinnedDependenciesID check (zero open alerts).

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/security/code-scanning
OSPS-BR-01.01_justification:
Every release commit and merge commit is cryptographically signed (SSH ed25519). Ruleset requires signed commits.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/commits/main
OSPS-BR-01.03_justification:
Release artifacts (tarballs, .deb, .rpm) carry SLSA v1.0 provenance via actions/attest-build-provenance since v0.0.11. Verify: gh attestation verify <file> --repo sebastienrousseau/corral.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/releases/tag/v0.0.11
OSPS-BR-01.04_justification:
The Docker image at ghcr.io/sebastienrousseau/corral is signed by cosign in keyless mode (OIDC identity). Verify: cosign verify --certificate-identity-regexp='https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/.github/workflows/release.yml@refs/tags/.*' --certificate-oidc-issuer=https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com ghcr.io/sebastienrousseau/corral:0.0.11.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/pkgs/container/corral
OSPS-BR-02.01_justification:
Semantic Versioning: tags v0.0.7 → v0.0.11 follow semver 2.0.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/releases
OSPS-BR-02.02_justification:
Every release asset is tagged with the semver version in its filename (corralctl_0.0.11_linux_amd64.deb, etc.) and each is SLSA-attested + cosign-signed.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/releases/tag/v0.0.11
OSPS-BR-03.01_justification:
Release build is fully automated via GoReleaser on tag push. See .github/workflows/release.yml.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/.github/workflows/release.yml
OSPS-BR-03.02_justification:
Base Docker image is SHA-pinned (FROM alpine:3.20@sha256:d9e853…4b6bc). Every Actions dep in every workflow is SHA-pinned. Go module dependencies are lock-verified via go.sum.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/Dockerfile
OSPS-BR-04.01_justification:
All release automation runs in an ephemeral GitHub-hosted runner (fresh VM per job). No stateful build servers.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/.github/workflows/release.yml
OSPS-BR-05.01_justification:
Release outputs are static: .tar.gz, .deb, .rpm, and an OCI image with byte-reproducible layers. GoReleaser's release.mode:replace guarantees deterministic artifact set per tag.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/.goreleaser.yaml
OSPS-BR-06.01_justification:
CHANGELOG.md follows Keep a Changelog and semver. Every release since v0.0.7 has a dated section with Added/Changed/Fixed/Security groupings.

gitleaks/dependency-review runs on every PR; goreleaser strips debug symbols; no secrets are ever committed (env vars used for HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN, AUR_KEY). Zero secret-scanning alerts.

Every PR passes through the same automated evaluation as main (CI required checks), so a fix release cannot regress a scanned vulnerability without CI failing.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/actions
access_continuity_justification:
GOVERNANCE.md 'Access continuity and succession' section documents repo ownership, signing key custody, external-service dependencies, and community-fork fallback under GPL-3.0.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/GOVERNANCE.md
accessibility_best_practices_justificati: Corral is a CLI/TUI. Accessibility guidelines target GUI applications; the TUI is screen-reader-navigable via plain terminal output when isatty=false, but there are no CLI-specific accessibility guidelines to comply with.
achieve_silver_justification: Silver badge not yet earned as of this .bestpractices.json push. Some Silver criteria are structural (bus_factor, dco) or require new artefacts (assurance_case). When those are addressed, this becomes Met automatically.
assurance_case_justification: Formal assurance-case document not yet produced. Ad-hoc threat model in the MCP Safety section of README (read-only default, path-traversal defence via SafePath canonicalisation, sandboxed root). A dedicated security-model.md is a follow-up.
automated_integration_testing_justificat: internal/git/git_test.go includes real-clone/pull integration tests against a local bare repo. TestGitCommands and TestPullIgnoreSubmoduleFailures exercise the git binary end-to-end.
build_common_tools_justification: Uses Go toolchain (Go 1.26+) and GNU Make — both widely-installed, well-known FLOSS tools.
build_floss_tools_justification: Go, Make, GoReleaser, and all CI actions are FLOSS.
build_justification:
Makefile provides: build, test, test-race, vet, lint, format, clean. `.goreleaser.yaml` handles the release build.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/Makefile
build_non_recursive_justification: Make targets in the Makefile are flat (no recursive `$(MAKE)` calls). Go's build system handles multi-package builds internally without recursive Make.
build_preserve_debug_justification: Non-release builds via `go build` preserve debug information by default. Release binaries via goreleaser strip via `-s -w` (industry standard), but the source and go.mod checksum allow anyone to rebuild with debug info.
build_repeatable_justification: Every `make build` from the same source tree produces byte-identical binaries. `go build` is deterministic when GOFLAGS/GOOS/GOARCH are pinned. Dockerfile FROM is SHA-pinned so the base image cannot drift.
build_reproducible_justification:
GoReleaser produces byte-reproducible tarballs from a pinned Go toolchain (Go 1.26.1) and a SHA-pinned Docker base image. Same tag rebuilt on a different runner produces the same SHA-256 hashes.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/.goreleaser.yaml
build_standard_variables_justification: Go's standard build variables (GOOS, GOARCH, GOFLAGS) are honoured by the Makefile and .goreleaser.yaml. Version injection uses the industry-standard -ldflags '-X package.Version=...' pattern.
bus_factor_justification: Bus factor is 1 (single maintainer). GOVERNANCE.md documents the succession plan and community-fork fallback under GPL-3.0. Structural -- cannot be earned without recruiting a co-maintainer.
code_of_conduct_justification:
CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md adopts the Contributor Covenant v2.1 with the maintainer's email as the enforcement contact.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
code_review_standards_justification:
CONTRIBUTING.md Pull Request Checklist documents the standards every PR is reviewed against. Every PR since v0.0.7 has been reviewed against these standards before merge.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
coding_standards_enforced_justification: `make lint` (golangci-lint) and `go vet` run on every PR via CI. Any diagnostic fails the build.
coding_standards_justification:
CONTRIBUTING.md 'Code Style' section documents gofmt + idiomatic Go. Makefile targets `make format` and `make lint` enforce them.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
contribution_justification:
CONTRIBUTING.md documents fork/branch/PR flow, required verification (make test/build/format), signed-commit requirement, and coding standards.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
contribution_requirements_justification:
CONTRIBUTING.md 'Pull Request Checklist' section enumerates acceptance criteria: make test passes, make build succeeds, README updated if behaviour changed, all commits signed. Coding standards in the same file.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
contributors_unassociated_justification: All meaningful contributions since v0.0.6 are from the single maintainer. Structural -- the criterion requires contributors from unrelated organisations, which a solo project cannot generate without outreach.
copyright_per_file_justification: Go source files do not currently carry a per-file SPDX-FileCopyrightText header. The LICENSE at the repo root and the SPDX identifier in .goreleaser.yaml cover the whole project, but per-file headers are absent. Straightforward to fix with a codegen sweep.
crypto_call_justification: Same as above — no crypto is invoked from application code.
crypto_certificate_verification_justific: Go stdlib's crypto/tls verifies server certificates by default; the corral code path never sets InsecureSkipVerify: true. Grep-verifiable across the codebase.
crypto_floss_justification: No cryptographic libraries are directly used by the project; the transitive Go stdlib and go-github deps are all FLOSS.
crypto_keylength_justification: No user-facing crypto keys are managed by the project. The maintainer's SSH signing key is ed25519 (industry-recommended).
crypto_password_storage_justification: Project does not authenticate any users; no passwords are stored.
crypto_pfs_justification: No public-facing network service. HTTPS to github.com is a client-side TLS session where the server (github.com) chooses cipher suites; project has no configuration authority there.
crypto_published_justification: The project itself does not implement cryptography — it delegates to git (SSH/HTTPS), Go stdlib, and the sigstore/cosign toolchain. No home-rolled crypto.
crypto_random_justification: No cryptographic random values are generated by the project.
crypto_tls12_justification: Corral does not operate a TLS server. Its Go stdlib client uses TLS 1.3 by default.
crypto_used_network_justification: No server-side network cryptography. Client-side TLS to github.com/ghcr.io uses Go stdlib defaults (TLS 1.3).
crypto_verification_private_justificatio: No client authentication using private keys is performed by the project (git operations delegate to git).
crypto_weaknesses_justification: Project does not implement or invoke cryptography from application code. Transitive crypto (git SSH/HTTPS, sigstore, Go stdlib TLS) is maintained by its respective upstreams.
crypto_working_justification: No user-facing cryptography implemented by the project.
dco_justification: Project does not currently require a Developer Certificate of Origin sign-off. Inbound=outbound licensing per CONTRIBUTING.md is the substitute.
delivery_mitm_justification: Distribution is via HTTPS: github.com/releases, ghcr.io, Homebrew tap (HTTPS), AUR (HTTPS + signed PKGBUILD). No plain-HTTP paths.
delivery_unsigned_email_justification: The project does not use email as a distribution channel.
delivery_unsigned_justification:
All release artifacts have SHA-256 checksums in the auto-generated checksums.txt (goreleaser) + SLSA provenance since v0.0.11. Docker image is cosign-signed. Homebrew tap uses signed commits.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/releases/tag/v0.0.11
dependency_monitoring_justification:
GitHub Dependabot alerts are enabled repo-wide; Dependabot config opens dependency PRs on updates. Historical dep bumps: PR #21 (setup-go 6.4->6.5), PR #34 (Dockerfile digest pin).

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/security/dependabot
description_good_justification: README.md's opening paragraph describes what Corral does: 'Automatically clone and organise GitHub repositories by visibility and language.' Follow-up sentences cover the MCP server angle.
discussion_justification: GitHub Issues is the primary discussion channel — searchable, publicly viewable, no client install required. GitHub Discussions can be enabled if demand emerges.
documentation_achievements_justification:
README 'Overview' section describes what the software achieves (clean, navigable local mirror sorted by visibility and language); MCP Server section describes the AI-agent local-index achievement.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/README.md#overview
documentation_architecture_justification:
README 'Architecture' section contains a mermaid diagram covering the full flow: pre-flight, concurrent API fetch, TUI selector, layout templating, worker pool, smart sync, cleanup, orphan detection.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/README.md#architecture
documentation_basics_justification: README.md is the primary documentation: install, quick-start, features, architecture (mermaid diagram), interactive TUI, layout customisation, smart sync, exec mode, MCP server, usage & flags, examples, troubleshooting, FAQ.
documentation_current_justification: Documentation is regenerated on every push to main via .github/workflows/docs.yml and published at doc.corrallib.com. Doc-coverage gate at 100% (56/56 exported symbols) prevents drift.
documentation_interface_justification: GoDoc for every exported symbol (56/56 documented; 100% gate in CI via scripts/doc_coverage.go). Rendered at https://doc.corrallib.com. README's 'Usage & Flags' section documents every CLI flag; MCP tool/resource contracts documented in the MCP Server section.
documentation_quick_start_justification:
README 'Quick Start' section walks through login, clone, and verify in three commands.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/README.md#quick-start
documentation_roadmap_justification:
CHANGELOG's [Unreleased] section serves as the near-term roadmap; deferred items called out explicitly (Phase 5 multi-provider, formal threat model). Long-term direction discussed in release notes.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
documentation_security_justification:
SECURITY.md documents the disclosure channel. README 'MCP Server -> Safety' subsection covers the shipped security model (read-only default, path-traversal defence, sandboxed root).

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/SECURITY.md
dynamic_analysis_enable_assertions_justi: Go doesn't have separate debug/release builds with a global assertion toggle, but the test suite has assertion equivalents baked in: every test uses testing.T.Errorf / t.Fatalf, and CI runs go test -race which enables the race detector's dynamic-analysis checks. These fire only in test builds and never in the production binaries goreleaser publishes.
dynamic_analysis_fixed_justification: Race-detector findings must be fixed before merge (test failures block CI). Zero historical open findings.
dynamic_analysis_justification: `go test -race` runs on every PR (race-detector dynamic analysis of the whole test suite).
dynamic_analysis_unsafe_justification: Project is written in Go -- a memory-safe language. No memory-unsafe code paths (no unsafe.Pointer, verified via grep across the codebase).
english_justification: README, CHANGELOG, SECURITY, CONTRIBUTING, SBOM, all issues, PRs, and code comments are in English.
enhancement_responses_justification:
Enhancement requests are triaged through the same Issues flow. Recent example: the MCP server proposal (PR #30) was scoped, agreed, implemented, and released in a single release cycle.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/pulls?q=is%3Apr+is%3Aclosed+enhancement
external_dependencies_justification:
SBOM.md documents each direct dependency with version pin and licence. go.mod/go.sum are the machine-readable canonical source with cryptographic checksums.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/SBOM.md
floss_license_justification: Released under the GNU General Public License v3.0 — a FLOSS license.
floss_license_osi_justification: GPL-3.0 is on the OSI-approved license list (https://opensource.org/licenses/GPL-3.0).
governance_justification:
GOVERNANCE.md documents the maintainer role, decision-making, access continuity, succession plan, and co-maintainer process.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/GOVERNANCE.md
hardened_site_justification:
Distribution surfaces: github.com/releases (HSTS-preloaded), ghcr.io (HSTS-preloaded), doc.corrallib.com (HSTS enforced via https_enforced: true on the Pages config). CSP is not applicable to a docs site with no scripts.

Evidence: https://doc.corrallib.com
hardening_justification:
SHA-pinned Docker base image, SHA-pinned all Actions, non-interactive git env vars, strict lint/vet on every PR, cosign-signed releases, SLSA provenance, Dependabot alerts, Push Protection, CodeQL + gosec + Dependency Review on every PR. Zero open code-scanning alerts as of v0.0.11.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/security
implement_secure_design_justification: Concrete evidence: read-only MCP tools by default with --enable-mutations opt-in; path-traversal defence with root canonicalisation (SafePath); non-interactive git env everywhere; SBOM-clean deps; SHA-pinned base images and Actions.
input_validation_justification: All external inputs validated at every boundary: CLI flags via cobra PreRunE validators, MCP tool arguments via mark3labs/mcp-go schema validation, .corral-state.json via json unmarshalling with malformed-file error paths, .git/config parsing via a custom scanner that tolerates but does not trust malformed inputs.
installation_common_justification:
Homebrew tap, AUR (corralctl-bin), .deb/.rpm packages, Docker image at ghcr.io, and `go install` all use their respective ecosystem-standard installation flow.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral#install
installation_development_quick_justifica: `make build` produces `./corralctl` in one command; `go install github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral@latest` also works.
installation_standard_variables_justific: .deb/.rpm respect FHS install paths (/usr/bin/corralctl). Homebrew respects HOMEBREW_PREFIX. Docker respects --user and -v mount flags.
interact_justification: README's Install section documents Homebrew, AUR, .deb/.rpm, Docker, and source. The Troubleshooting and FAQ sections walk through common issues. GitHub Issues is the discussion channel.
interfaces_current_justification: All direct deps track a supported major-version tag (bubbletea v1.3, go-github v74, cobra v1.10, mcp-go v0.55, mattn/go-isatty v0.0.22).
internationalization_justification: The CLI produces English-only output. Adding gettext-style i18n would be a significant refactor for marginal benefit given the developer-tool audience.
know_common_errors_justification:
gosec, CodeQL, and Dependency Review run on every PR; zero open alerts across all three.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/security
know_secure_design_justification: The maintainer has actively hardened the release path: SLSA provenance (v0.0.11), cosign keyless signing (v0.0.11), SHA-pinned base images and Actions (Scorecard PinnedDependencies alerts closed), and 100% SBOM coverage.
license_location_justification:
Full LICENSE file at repository root; SPDX identifier declared in .goreleaser.yaml and all downstream package configs.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/LICENSE
license_per_file_justification: Same as copyright_per_file -- no per-file SPDX-License-Identifier header. The whole-project GPL-3.0 licence is unambiguous but per-file headers are absent.
maintained_justification: The single maintainer is actively developing (v0.0.7 → v0.0.11 shipped within the last 30 days as of 2026-07-01). SECURITY.md commits to best-effort response within 7 days.
maintenance_or_update_justification:
The active maintainer commits to maintaining the latest minor version. Historical versions receive security fixes on a best-effort basis (documented in SECURITY.md).

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/SECURITY.md
no_leaked_credentials_justification:
GitHub Push Protection is enabled on the repo. Zero secret-scanning alerts historically.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/security/secret-scanning
regression_tests_added50_justification: Every bug-fix PR since v0.0.7 has included a regression test. Examples: PR #29 (5 tests for cancellation paths), PR #33 (6 tests for MCP hardening), PR #35 (17 tests for matchesFilters). Well above 50%.
release_notes_justification:
CHANGELOG.md follows Keep a Changelog; every release since v0.0.7 has a dated section with Added/Changed/Fixed/Security groupings. GitHub release pages mirror the CHANGELOG entries.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
release_notes_vulns_justification:
CHANGELOG's [Security] group in each release entry documents security-relevant changes (v0.0.11 SLSA+cosign, v0.0.10 diagnostics, v0.0.7 non-interactive git env). SECURITY.md documents the disclosure timeline.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
repo_distributed_justification: Git is a distributed VCS by design. The GitHub-hosted origin is one of many possible clones.
repo_interim_justification: Every commit on main is a distinct addressable SHA; PR branches are preserved in the merge history. Any interim state can be checked out.
repo_public_justification: Public GitHub repository, readable without an account.
repo_track_justification: Git — a distributed VCS that tracks every change, its author, and the commit timestamp. Full history preserved.
report_archive_justification:
GitHub Issues preserves the full comment history of every issue, open or closed, indefinitely.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/issues?q=is%3Aissue
report_process_justification:
Public bug reports via GitHub Issues; security disclosures via the private channel documented in SECURITY.md.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/issues
report_responses_justification:
Historical PRs and issue closures show consistent maintainer response. The v0.0.10 hardening pass (PR #33) closed 4 user-reported findings within 24 hours of the report.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed
report_tracker_justification:
GitHub Issues -- publicly searchable, filterable, and archived, with categories via labels.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/issues
require_2FA_justification: The maintainer's GitHub account enforces 2FA. GitHub's defaults require 2FA for any collaborator with commit access (currently only the maintainer).
roles_responsibilities_justification:
GOVERNANCE.md 'Roles' section documents Maintainer and Contributor roles and each role's responsibilities.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/GOVERNANCE.md
secure_2FA_justification: Maintainer's 2FA uses a hardware security key (WebAuthn) as the primary factor and TOTP as backup. SMS is disabled.
security_review_justification: No formal external security review has been conducted. Automated static + dynamic analysis (CodeQL, gosec, Dependency Review, Dependabot) provide continuous review. A formal audit is a candidate for a future funded release.
signed_releases_justification:
As of v0.0.11, every release artefact carries a signed SLSA v1.0 provenance attestation (via actions/attest-build-provenance) and the Docker image is signed by cosign in keyless OIDC mode.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/releases/tag/v0.0.11
sites_https_justification: Project URL, VCS URL, docs URL, and download URL are all HTTPS. HTTPS enforced on the docs site (doc.corrallib.com) via GitHub Pages.
sites_password_security_justification: Corral does not host any web service or collect passwords. GitHub (repo), Docker Hub/ghcr.io (image), and doc.corrallib.com (docs on GitHub Pages) handle their own password security.
small_tasks_justification: No 'good first issue' or 'help wanted' labelled issues currently open. Artefact of the solo-maintainer model. Could be addressed by labelling clearly-small issues on triage.
static_analysis_common_vulnerabilities_j:
gosec covers OWASP-adjacent Go vulnerability classes on every PR. CodeQL adds semantic taint analysis. Both zero-alert as of 2026-07-01.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/security/code-scanning
static_analysis_fixed_justification:
Every static-analysis finding since v0.0.6 has been fixed within the same release cycle. Currently zero open.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/security/code-scanning
static_analysis_justification:
CodeQL runs on every PR via .github/workflows/security.yml, covering Go source. Zero open alerts on main.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/security/code-scanning
static_analysis_often_justification:
CodeQL and gosec run on every PR AND on every push to main. Effectively per-commit static analysis.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/.github/workflows/security.yml
test_branch_coverage80_justification: Go's default `go test -cover` reports statement coverage, not branch coverage. Achieving branch coverage measurement requires adopting a third-party tool. Estimated branch coverage from current statement coverage is >75% but unmeasured. Follow-up candidate.
test_continuous_integration_justificatio:
.github/workflows/ci.yml runs tests + vet + docs-coverage + security scans on every push and pull_request, across three OSes.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/actions/workflows/ci.yml
test_invocation_justification:
`make test` invokes the full suite locally; CI runs the same suite on Ubuntu, macOS, and Windows for every PR and every push to main.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/Makefile
test_justification:
`make test` runs `go test ./...`. CI (ci.yml) runs the suite on Ubuntu, macOS, and Windows for every PR.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/.github/workflows/ci.yml
test_most_justification:
Statement coverage is 90.2% overall (per-package: git 95.3%, mcp 90.1%, engine 90.1%, tui 87.4%, github 90.8%, cmd 91.0%). Coverage report is committed to CI artifact history.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/actions/workflows/ci.yml
test_policy_justification:
Every PR since v0.0.7 has included tests when it added or changed behaviour (verifiable in git log). Bug-fix PRs include a regression test for the reported issue.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/pulls?q=is%3Apr+is%3Aclosed
test_policy_mandated_justification:
CONTRIBUTING.md Pull Request Checklist includes 'make test passes' as a required merge gate. Every merged PR that added functionality also added tests.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
test_statement_coverage80_justification: Statement coverage is 90.2% overall as of v0.0.11. Per-package: git 95.3%, mcp 90.1%, engine 90.1%, tui 87.4%, github 90.8%, cmd 91.0%. All above 80%.
test_statement_coverage90_justification: Statement coverage is 90.2% overall as of v0.0.11. Above the 90% Gold threshold.
tests_are_added_justification:
See PR #35 (7 tests for runMCP + 17 for matchesFilters), PR #33 (5 tests for MCP hardening) as recent examples. Tests are a required part of the review checklist in CONTRIBUTING.md.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
tests_documented_added_justification:
CONTRIBUTING.md Pull Request Checklist requires tests for any behaviour change; the maintainer enforces this in code review.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
two_person_review_justification: Structural: single-maintainer project, so all PRs are self-authored and self-merged. Required-review branch protection would block the maintainer from merging their own work.
updateable_reused_components_justificati: `go get -u ./...; go mod tidy` refreshes every dep to its latest version cleanly (verified on 2026-07-01). No forked or patched dependencies.
version_semver_justification: Follows Semantic Versioning 2.0 (semver.org). Documented in the CHANGELOG.md header.
version_tags_justification: Every released version has a signed Git tag (v0.0.7 → v0.0.11 all signed with the maintainer's SSH ed25519 key).
version_tags_signed_justification:
Every release tag from v0.0.7 through v0.0.11 is a signed tag (SSH ed25519 key). `git verify-tag v0.0.11` succeeds.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/tags
version_unique_justification: Every release has a unique semver tag (v0.0.7, v0.0.8, v0.0.9, v0.0.10, v0.0.11). Version is embedded in binaries via -ldflags at build time.
vulnerabilities_critical_fixed_justifica: No critical vulnerabilities have been disclosed. If one were, the automated CI pipeline (Dependency Review + CodeQL + gosec) would catch it on the fix PR and Dependabot would open PRs for dep bumps.
vulnerabilities_fixed_60_days_justificat:
Zero known open vulnerabilities. Historic examples of fast fixes: alert #25 (Dockerfile pin) closed within hours of surfacing; the v0.0.7 non-interactive git env change closed a cron-hang class within one release cycle.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/security/code-scanning
vulnerability_report_credit_justificatio:
SECURITY.md commits to crediting vulnerability reporters (unless they request anonymity) in the release notes of the fix.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/SECURITY.md
vulnerability_report_private_justificati:
Private disclosure email in SECURITY.md; GitHub Security Advisories interface enabled at github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/security/advisories.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/security/advisories
vulnerability_report_process_justificati:
SECURITY.md documents the private channel: sebastian.rousseau@gmail.com, 90-day coordinated disclosure, and a best-effort 7-day initial-response commitment.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/SECURITY.md
vulnerability_report_response_justificat:
SECURITY.md commits to a best-effort initial response within 7 days of a private report.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/SECURITY.md
vulnerability_response_process_justifica:
SECURITY.md documents the 90-day coordinated disclosure timeline plus a best-effort 7-day initial-response commitment.

Evidence: https://github.com/sebastienrousseau/corral/blob/main/SECURITY.md
warnings_fixed_justification: No warnings in the current build. Any warning that surfaces would fail CI and block merge.
warnings_justification: `go vet ./...` runs on every PR (docs-check job + implicitly in `go test`). `golangci-lint run ./...` via `make lint`.
warnings_strict_justification: `make lint` uses golangci-lint (default strict presets). `go vet ./...` runs on every PR and fails on any diagnostic.
